classDescription: A divine conduit of a deity's power.
roleIdentity: Durable support caster with healing, buffs, and radiant control.

cleric-spellcasting.summary: Prepare and wield divine magic from the full cleric list using Wisdom as your casting ability.
cleric-spellcasting.description:
Cleric spellcasting is the backbone of the class. You prepare spells from the cleric list each day, use Wisdom for your spell attacks and save DC, and pivot between healing, protection, control, and radiant pressure depending on what the party needs.

In play, this should feel flexible rather than narrow: the same cleric can solve attrition with healing, anchor the front line with buffs, or answer undead and hostile magic with the right prepared list.

cleric-divine-order.summary: Choose the side of clerical training you want to emphasize so the class starts with a clearer battlefield and role identity.
cleric-divine-order.description:
Divine Order is the first big fork in how your cleric plays. It decides whether your build leans harder into scholarship, discipline, and sacred study or into a more armored, battle-ready priest style.

This choice should be visible in the sheet because it affects how the player understands the class from level 1 onward: support caster, battle priest, or a hybrid of both.

cleric-channel-divinity-1-use.summary: Spend Channel Divinity to power major cleric or domain effects when the moment really matters.
cleric-channel-divinity-1-use.description:
Channel Divinity is your premier class resource. It fuels signature cleric buttons such as Divine Spark, Turn Undead, and subclass-specific options like Radiance of the Dawn or Invoke Duplicity.

In play, this should feel like a high-value tactical choice rather than background text. The player should always understand how many uses remain, which actions consume a use, and whether the current fight is worth spending one now.
cleric-channel-divinity-1-use.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Short or Long Rest.

cleric-divine-spark.summary: Release focused divine power to either restore an ally or punish a target with radiant or necrotic force.
cleric-divine-spark.description:
Divine Spark is your dependable Channel Divinity option when you need immediate impact. It turns the class resource into direct healing or damage instead of a passive aura or setup effect.

Use it when a single creature needs to swing now: lift an ally, finish a threatening target, or force a visible turn in momentum without spending a spell slot.
cleric-divine-spark.effect: Immediate healing or divine damage with no spell-slot cost.

cleric-turn-undead.summary: Drive undead back with holy force and break the shape of an encounter that depends on them closing in.
cleric-turn-undead.description:
Turn Undead is a battlefield control Channel Divinity, not just lore flavor. Against undead-heavy encounters it can buy space, protect weaker allies, and stop the enemy line from playing the fight it wanted.

This feature matters most when the cleric reads the room early and spends it before the undead horde has already collapsed onto the party.
cleric-turn-undead.effect: Undead creatures that fail are repelled and lose tempo.

cleric-divine-domain-subclass.summary: Your chosen domain defines what kind of cleric you really are and which signature powers start shaping the build.
cleric-divine-domain-subclass.description:
Divine Domain is the subclass gate for cleric. It is where the class stops being a generic divine caster and becomes a Light cleric, Trickery cleric, War cleric, or Life cleric with a distinct play pattern.

From this point on, the sheet should surface domain actions, passive riders, and any domain-specific uses of Channel Divinity clearly enough that the subclass feels like a real identity shift.

cleric-blessed-strikes.summary: Your regular offense gains a reliable divine rider so your turns stay meaningful even when you are not spending top resources.
cleric-blessed-strikes.description: Blessed Strikes keeps the cleric’s baseline pressure relevant by adding divine payoff to weapon attacks or cantrip damage. It matters because not every turn is a perfect high-level spell turn; sometimes you need dependable value while preserving slots.

cleric-divine-intervention.summary: Call on your deity for a dramatic answer when the situation is too important for ordinary magic to cover alone.
cleric-divine-intervention.description:
Divine Intervention is the cleric fantasy spike. It should read like a momentous plea for direct aid rather than a generic spell-like button.

In play, it belongs in boss fights, desperate rescues, or scenes where the party needs more than efficient math and wants the cleric to ask for something greater than routine casting.

cleric-greater-divine-intervention.summary: Your god answers with far greater certainty, turning Divine Intervention from a gamble into a true endgame class payoff.
cleric-greater-divine-intervention.description: Greater Divine Intervention is the high-level reward for a lifetime of service. By this point the cleric should feel like a chosen instrument of divine power, and the sheet should treat this as a major class-defining button rather than a footnote at level 20.

cleric-spell-level-2.summary: Unlock 2nd-level spell slots, expanding your prepared list into stronger healing, control, and offensive options.
cleric-spell-level-2.description: 2nd-level spells open access to powerful cleric options: Spiritual Weapon, Hold Person, Silence, Aid, and Enhance Ability. Your prepared list grows with your level, so higher-tier spells become available without giving up access to 1st-level slots.

cleric-spell-level-3.summary: Unlock 3rd-level spell slots — a significant jump in available spell power and battlefield control options.
cleric-spell-level-3.description: 3rd-level spells are a major spike for the cleric: Dispel Magic, Revivify, Spirit Guardians, Mass Healing Word, and Bestow Curse are all available here. Proficiency bonus also increases to +3 at this level.

cleric-spell-level-4.summary: Unlock 4th-level spell slots and expand access to stronger domain expressions and defensive options.
cleric-spell-level-4.description: 4th-level spells include Banishment, Death Ward, Freedom of Movement, Guardian of Faith, and Stone Shape. Your expanded prepared list can now address a wider variety of threats.

cleric-spell-level-5.summary: Unlock 5th-level spell slots — the tier where cleric spellcasting reaches its most powerful general-purpose options.
cleric-spell-level-5.description: 5th-level spells are a turning point: Flame Strike, Mass Cure Wounds, Raise Dead, Scrying, and Commune are all available. Proficiency bonus increases to +4. This is often the level where the cleric's support and control output feels definitively powerful.

cleric-spell-level-6.summary: Unlock 6th-level spell slots, extending into rare divine magic with powerful control and recovery effects.
cleric-spell-level-6.description: 6th-level spells open Heal, Harm, Blade Barrier, Find the Path, and True Seeing. At this tier, single spell slots can swing an entire encounter.

cleric-spell-level-7.summary: Unlock 7th-level spell slots and access to some of the most powerful divine magic available.
cleric-spell-level-7.description: 7th-level spells include Conjure Celestial, Divine Word, Plane Shift, and Resurrection. Proficiency bonus increases to +5.

cleric-spell-level-8.summary: Unlock 8th-level spell slots and a near-pinnacle tier of divine power.
cleric-spell-level-8.description: 8th-level spells include Antimagic Field, Control Weather, Earthquake, and Holy Aura. These spells can reshape the battlefield entirely.

cleric-spell-level-9.summary: Unlock 9th-level spell slots — the highest tier of spellcasting, available to very few beings in the world.
cleric-spell-level-9.description: 9th-level spells include True Resurrection, Mass Heal, Gate, and Wish (if on the list). Proficiency bonus increases to +6. A single 9th-level spell can fundamentally alter the course of a campaign moment.

cleric-channel-divinity-2-uses.summary: Channel Divinity now refreshes to 2 uses per rest, giving you significantly more resource flexibility.
cleric-channel-divinity-2-uses.description: With 2 Channel Divinity uses, you can spend one early in a fight and still have a second for the domain option or Turn Undead when the moment demands it. Recovery still happens on a short or long rest.
cleric-channel-divinity-2-uses.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Short or Long Rest.

cleric-channel-divinity-3-uses.summary: Channel Divinity now provides 3 uses per rest — enough to be a consistent tactical tool across every fight.
cleric-channel-divinity-3-uses.description: With 3 Channel Divinity uses per rest, the high-level cleric can treat this resource as a reliable part of every encounter, not just a once-per-fight emergency button. Domain Channel Divinity options become much more deployable.
cleric-channel-divinity-3-uses.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Short or Long Rest.

cleric-cantrips-known-4.summary: You now know 4 cleric cantrips, giving you a broader zero-slot toolkit.
cleric-cantrips-known-4.description: A fourth cantrip slot lets you round out your cantrip coverage: typically one offensive option (Sacred Flame or Word of Radiance), one utility (Guidance, Light, Thaumaturgy), and one situational pick.

cleric-cantrips-known-5.summary: You now know 5 cleric cantrips — a full set covering offense, utility, and defense.
cleric-cantrips-known-5.description: 5 cantrips is the Cleric's maximum. By level 10, this should represent a well-rounded set that covers all major zero-slot scenarios your build needs.

cleric-ability-score-improvement-4.summary: Improve two ability scores by 1 each, or take a feat to add a specialized capability.
cleric-ability-score-improvement-4.description: Most Cleric builds increase Wisdom first (to improve spell save DC, healing rolls, and spell attacks) or Constitution (to improve HP and Concentration saves). Feats like War Caster or Resilient (Constitution) are strong options for Concentration-dependent builds.

cleric-ability-score-improvement-8.summary: Second ASI or feat — continue improving Wisdom or Constitution, or add a feat that suits your domain identity.
cleric-ability-score-improvement-8.description: By level 8, consider whether your primary stats are high enough or if a feat adds more value. Spell Sniper, Inspiring Leader, or additional saving throw proficiency can all be strong picks depending on your domain and party role.

cleric-ability-score-improvement-12.summary: Third ASI or feat — shore up weaknesses or maximize your most impactful stat.
cleric-ability-score-improvement-12.description: If Wisdom or Constitution are not yet at their target scores, this is the level to close the gap. Alternatively, a utility feat can add out-of-combat flexibility or defense.

cleric-ability-score-improvement-16.summary: Fourth ASI or feat — high-level refinement of your cleric build.
cleric-ability-score-improvement-16.description: By level 16, most primary stats are maximized. This is a natural point for a feat that rewards the build you have been running rather than the one you planned at level 1.

cleric-subclass-feature-6.summary: Your Divine Domain unlocks a second defining feature, deepening the domain's mechanical and thematic identity.
cleric-subclass-feature-6.description: The level 6 domain feature is a significant step in your domain's power curve. Life gains Blessed Healer, Light gains Improved Flare, Trickery gains Cloak of Shadows, War gains War God's Blessing. Each transforms the domain from an early-game identity into a sustained tactical asset.

cleric-subclass-feature-8.summary: Your Divine Domain grants a passive or active feature that reinforces its offense, defense, or support identity.
cleric-subclass-feature-8.description: The level 8 domain feature for most domains is Divine Strike (extra weapon damage) or Potent Spellcasting (extra cantrip damage). These features ensure the domain stays relevant in the baseline action economy between major spell turns.

cleric-subclass-feature-14.summary: Your Divine Domain does not grant a feature at level 14 in this progression — this level is covered by the progression table.
cleric-subclass-feature-14.description: Level 14 is a Subclass Feature placeholder. Verify against your specific domain's roadmap; some domains grant features at different tiers than others.

cleric-subclass-feature-17.summary: Your Divine Domain unlocks its capstone feature, completing the domain's full mechanical identity.
cleric-subclass-feature-17.description: The level 17 domain feature is the final expression of the domain's identity. Life gains Supreme Healing, Light gains Corona of Light, Trickery gains Improved Duplicity, War gains Avatar of Battle. These capstones are significant power jumps that define how the domain plays at high levels.

cleric-epic-boon.summary: Choose an Epic Boon feat — a rare, powerful enhancement available only to characters at this level.
cleric-epic-boon.description: Epic Boons are very powerful feats gated behind high level play. They provide abilities beyond the normal scope of feat choices. Select the one that best matches your cleric's role and the campaign's remaining challenges.

progressionSummary.summary: Channel Divinity (1 use), Divine Spark, Turn Undead.
Ability Score Improvement, Subclass Feature, Blessed Strikes.

buildTips.primaryReason: powers your spells, healing, and save DC
buildTips.secondaryReason: improves survivability and Concentration on key buffs
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: even armored clerics benefit from initiative and Dexterity saves
